tests_not_run: 原始报告为只读审查输出；未运行项保留在 finding.required_tests。

findings.title: Full governance test step can write pytest and Python caches inside the checkout

evidence.result: The scheduled/manual-all test step runs python3 -m pytest tests/governance -q or python3 -m unittest discover directly from the repository checkout.
Only the log file and generated dashboard output are redirected to RUNNER_TEMP; no Python bytecode or pytest cache location is redirected.
No PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE, PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX, PYTEST_ADDOPTS, or -p no:cacheprovider controls were present; pytest/unittest commands were present on lines 55 and 57.
evidence.command: Node targeted search for PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE, PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX, cacheprovider, pytest, unittest

evidence.result: The standard says READ_ONLY/REVIEW/CI/Hook writes are prohibited and read-only modes, CI, and Hook must write nothing.

reproduction: NOT_RUN: Running the default workflow pytest/unittest command was intentionally skipped because it is the suspected cache-writing action under RUN_MODE=READ_ONLY.

Static reproduction: inspect .github/workflows/project-governance.yml and verify the test step lacks PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1, PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX outside the checkout, and pytest cacheprovider disablement.
After a fix, run the scheduled/manual-all test command in a clean checkout and assert git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all remains empty while caches, if any, are under RUNNER_TEMP.
findings.impact: The full governance path is documented as non-writing, but the test runner can create .pytest_cache and __pycache__ in the repository checkout. That weakens zero-write evidence and can pollute later changed-scope or dirty-tree checks, especially in local or reused workspaces.
findings.recommended_fix: At workflow/job level set PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 and PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX to a RUNNER_TEMP directory, disable pytest cache with PYTEST_ADDOPTS=-p no:cacheprovider or an explicit pytest flag, and add a final git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all assertion for the full governance path.
required_tests: NOT_RUN: default pytest/unittest cache-write reproduction in the repository checkout, skipped due READ_ONLY.
Add/extend a workflow text test asserting PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE, PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX, and pytest cacheprovider disablement are present for the full governance test step.
Add a CI/local integration check that runs the full governance test command with cache controls and verifies git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all is empty.

findings.title: Workflow-level cancellation can preempt scheduled or manual full-governance evidence runs

evidence.result: The workflow has schedule and workflow_dispatch triggers but uses one concurrency group based only on github.ref with cancel-in-progress: true.

Short PR/push changed-only runs and long schedule/manual all runs share the same workflow-level cancellation policy even though only the latter produce full governance/generated-view/attestation evidence.
Generated views and CI attestation are uploaded only after successful full runs; a canceled full run has no guaranteed final artifact.

reproduction: NOT_RUN: Did not trigger GitHub Actions runs in READ_ONLY mode.

On the same ref, start a manual workflow_dispatch scope=all run, then push to the same ref or start another run before it completes; with the current group project-governance-${{ github.ref }}, the in-progress full run is eligible for cancellation.
Observe whether generated governance views or attestation artifacts are absent after cancellation.
findings.impact: A main push or another same-ref run can cancel scheduled/manual full governance before it uploads generated views or attestation. This can starve lifecycle evidence and makes nightly/manual proof unreliable under active development.
findings.recommended_fix: Split concurrency by event and scope, or disable cancel-in-progress for schedule and workflow_dispatch scope=all. For example, use separate groups for changed-only fast gates and full evidence gates, and keep cancellation only for PR/push fast gates.
required_tests: Add a workflow text test that scheduled/manual all runs do not share a cancelable concurrency group with PR/push changed-only runs.
NOT_RUN: live GitHub cancellation race test, skipped because triggering workflows is prohibited in READ_ONLY.
After fixing, perform a controlled workflow_dispatch scope=all plus push overlap test and verify the full run either completes or preserves a cancellation artifact/summary.

findings.title: Diff-selection git subprocesses lack per-command timeouts

evidence.result: git_ref_exists and git_changed_files call subprocess.run/check_output for rev-parse, diff, and ls-files without timeout arguments.
git_status_porcelain already uses timeout=5.0, showing the codebase has a local pattern for bounded git calls that diff selection does not follow.
The workflow has only a coarse 10 minute job timeout, so a hung diff-selection command can consume the whole job without structured validator output.

reproduction: NOT_RUN: Did not simulate a hung git binary or repository lock in READ_ONLY mode.
Patch or monkeypatch subprocess.check_output in git_changed_files to raise TimeoutExpired and observe current code has no explicit timeout handling path.
Run changed-only validation against a repository state where git diff or git ls-files hangs; the current implementation depends on the outer job timeout.
findings.impact: Cancellation and timeout behavior is too coarse for the first diff-selection step. If git blocks, the governance command may hang until the whole CI job is killed, losing structured JSON/log evidence and slowing PR feedback.
findings.recommended_fix: Introduce a shared bounded git helper with a small timeout for rev-parse, diff, and ls-files. Convert timeout, missing base, and command failure into explicit validation errors surfaced in the JSON/summary instead of relying on the workflow job timeout.
required_tests: Add unit tests monkeypatching subprocess.run/check_output to raise TimeoutExpired for git_ref_exists and git_changed_files, asserting nonzero validation and a clear error message.
Add a test proving valid git commands still return the same changed-file set within the timeout helper.
NOT_RUN: real hung-git stress test, skipped under READ_ONLY.

findings.title: changed-scope diagnostic command reports success for an unresolved base ref

evidence.result: build_changed_scope returns a JSON summary and main returns 0 without checking whether an explicit base_ref resolved.
git_changed_files only adds the base diff if git_ref_exists(explicit_base) is true; an unresolved explicit base silently falls through to local working-tree changes.
Exited successfully and printed changed_files=[], selected_project_count=0, total_project_count=10 for an impossible base ref.
The actual ci entrypoint returned status=1 and embedded validation.exit_code=1, so the defect is limited to the changed-scope diagnostic path rather than the workflow ci gate.
evidence.command: Node spawnSync python scripts/lean_governance.py ci --changed-only --base-ref refs/heads/__codex_missing_base_ref__

reproduction: Run: python scripts/lean_governance.py changed-scope --base-ref refs/heads/__codex_missing_base_ref__. The command prints selected_project_count=0 instead of failing or marking base_ref_resolved=false.
Control: run the ci entrypoint with the same base ref; it fails through enforce-sync, proving the diagnostic command and CI command disagree on result validity.
findings.impact: Manual operators and review agents can misread an unresolved diff base as a genuine 0-project changed-scope result. That undermines stress testing of 0/1/multi-project selection even though the main CI entrypoint catches the same case.
findings.recommended_fix: Make changed-scope validate an explicit base_ref before computing selected projects. If unresolved, return nonzero and include a structured base_ref_resolved=false error; alternatively delegate base validation to the same helper used by enforce-sync.
required_tests: Add a unit test for lean_governance changed-scope with an explicit missing base ref that expects nonzero or a structured unresolved-base error.
Keep the existing ci missing-base test/control to ensure ci and changed-scope agree on result validity.
Run the fixed command against a real base ref and verify selected_project_count remains correct for 0, 1, and multiple project changes.
